data.title: "Don't Ignore This," Morgan Stanley Analyst Says UBER Stock Remains Undervalued
data.description:
Uber (UBER) is drawing fresh attention this week after Morgan Stanley analyst Brian Nowak said the stock looks undervalued. The analyst reiterated his Buy rating and kept a $110 price target, stating that the market is underestimating the strength of Uber's core U.S. mobility business.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Uber's Rideshare Unit Priced Too Low vs. Lyft, Says Nowak

Nowak believes that Uber's U.S. rideshare segment is being priced at a steep discount to peers, despite solid fundamentals. His sum‑of‑the‑parts analysis suggests that at around $85 per share, the market is valuing Uber's U.S. mobility unit at roughly 7x projected 2027 adjusted EBITDA, a level he says is far too low for a business growing in the mid-teens.

He also noted that this valuation is more than 10% below Lyft's (LYFT) multiple, even though Uber is larger, more profitable, and better diversified.

In 2026, Nowak expects a flood of autonomous vehicle (AV) news and pilot programs, which could create volatility for both Uber and Lyft. Still, he believes AV developments will have minimal impact on Uber's financials over the next 12 to 24 months, reinforcing his view that the stock's current discount is unjustified.

BTIG analyst Jake Fuller echoed the optimism, with a Buy rating and a $100 price target. He called Uber one of the strongest growth stories in consumer internet, supported by steady bookings growth, improving margins, and ongoing buybacks. Fuller also argued that AV-related risks are overstated and already reflected in Uber's valuation.

Is Uber a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on UBER stock based on 29 Buys, three Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months. Further, the average Uber price target of $113.93 per share implies 35.02% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: BYDDY, XPEV, NIO: Chinese EV Makers in Focus as Canada, China Strike Tariff Deal
data.description:
Chinese electric-vehicle makers such as BYD (BYDDY), XPeng (XPEV), and Nio (NIO) may be gearing up for a boost. On Friday, Canada and China reset their diplomatic ties, agreeing to an early tariff cut on electric vehicles.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Mark Carney, Canada's prime minister, on Friday said his administration will impose a 6.1% tariff on EVs from China, allowing up to 49,000 units into the North American country. This marks a dramatic turnaround from the 100% tariffs imposed on the Asian giant by Justin Trudeau, Canada's former prime minister, in 2024.

The arrangement suggests that Canada — which counts the U.S. as its largest trading partner, followed by China — may be seeking other strategic alignments, even as diplomatic relations with Washington have taken a hit recently. In May 2024, the U.S., under former President Joe Biden, imposed a 100% tariff on Chinese EV imports, with the duty coming into effect that same year.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: 'Markets Climb Wall-of-Worry': Oppenheimer Sees Resilience Driving 2026 and Suggests 2 Stocks to Buy
data.description:
The S&P 500 wrapped up 2025 on a strong note, shaking off a few bumps along the way. The Q3 growth numbers helped reinforce that momentum, with the economy expanding at a 4.3% annualized pace. Consumer spending played a big role, rising 3.5%, while exports also chipped in. The December jobs report delivered a softer-than-expected finish, with job gains slowing and unemployment edging down to 4.4%, adding to evidence of cooling growth rather than signaling a hard turn in labor conditions.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That kind of backdrop helps explain why some strategists are still leaning positive. John Stoltzfus, Oppenheimer's chief investment strategist, is one of them.

"Considering the resilience that the market showed last year," Stoltzfus says, "and the capability it showed to rally on economic, corporate, and consumer fundamentals it looks to us like the future as uncertain as it always is persists in having opportunities for investors with right-sized expectations of how various asset classes and the businesses tied to them navigate change. Revenue and earnings growth and innovation supported by sensible monetary policy are likely to support economic growth at a sustainable pace in 2026. Resilience will remain the operative word to make it happen and carry the markets forward providing 'the oomph' to climb the proverbial 'wall of worry.'"

The Oppenheimer stock analysts are following Stoltzfus' path, and looking for stocks that can gain as resilience drives the markets. They've picked two, and we've used the TipRanks platform to see how the Oppenheimer view compares to the Street's consensus.

Amprius Technologies (AMPX)

The first Oppenheimer pick we'll look at is Amprius Technologies, a company that is positioning itself to take a leading role as the economy shifts more toward the use of electric power. Amprius specializes in the design and manufacture of high-energy-density, high-capacity lithium-ion batteries that make use of silicon anodes. Energy density is a key metric in battery technology, as it measures how much power is available to the device using the storage cell.

Amprius boasts that it can deliver batteries with the highest energy density on the market, along with a long life cycle and ultra-fast charging times. The company backs this up with safety and reliability – making sure that its batteries have passed rigorous checks that include the U.S. military's nail penetration test. We've all heard the horror stories of lithium battery fires – Amprius makes sure that its batteries are as safe as possible.

The company's batteries offer 100% longer running times, a -30 to +60 degree centigrade operating temperature range, more than 1,300 lifetime charge cycles, and a mere 6-minute charging time to 80% capacity. The batteries are designed for a wide range of applications, including manned and unmanned aircraft, light electric vehicles such as e-bikes, and AI and robotics. Amprius maintains its headquarters in Fremont, California, and has four manufacturing facilities in East Asia.

In addition to its role in an increasingly important sector, Amprius is in the midst of a leadership transition. As of January 1, new CEO Tom Stepien has taken the helm. Stepien had been President of the company since May of last year. Former CEO Dr. Kang Sun will remain affiliated with Amprius, and will be present as an Executive Advisor.

In its 3Q25 report, Amprius declared a top line of $21.4 million, a record for the company. The Q3 revenue beat the forecast by $4.6 million, and was up a whopping 172% year-over-year. At the bottom line, Amprius ran a net loss of 3 cents per share – but even this was 3 cents per share better than had been expected. We should note that Amprius' stock is up 187.5% in the last 12 months.

Top analyst Colin Rusch, who is rated among the top 1% of Wall Street's analysts, notes the new CEO and the strong manufacturing and goes on from there. He writes of Amprius, "As AMPX makes a CEO transition with President Tom Stepien taking over at CEO, we believe the company is entering a new phase of its development. With new financial leadership in place as well as 2GWH+ of manufacturing capacity available, including several new manufacturers and associated supply chain partners meeting the requirements of the US National Defense Authorization Act ("NDAA"), we expect the company to focus on a long-term pricing strategy intended to maximize value capture as it transitions customers from sampling to production orders… We continue to believe AMPX has a multi-year lead on high-density, lightweight battery technology for aerospace/defense applications and is poised to announce significant new customers."

This stance earns AMPX stock an Outperform (i.e., Buy) rating from Rusch, while his $17 price target implies a one-year gain of 67.5%. (To watch Rusch's track record, click here.)

This stock gets a Strong Buy from the analyst consensus, based on 7 unanimously positive reviews. The stock is priced at $10.15, and its $17.17 average target price indicates an upside of 69% for the year ahead. (See AMPX stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: Hyundai Poaches Former Tesla Robotics Guru as it Maps Out Atlas Humanoid Growth
data.description:
Hyundai Motor Group (HYMLF) stepped up its push into humanoid robotics today after poaching the former vice president in charge of Tesla's (TSLA) Optimus robot.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Hyundai Hires Kovac

The Korean firm has appointed Milan Kovac as a group adviser and outside director at its U.S.-based robotics subsidiary Boston Dynamics.

Kovac, who has nearly two decades of experience working across software, hardware and AI-driven robotics, worked at Tesla for around nine years before leaving in June. He was the leader of a number of major projects at the Elon Musk firm, including its humanoid robot Optimus. He also played a part in the development of camera-based, vision-centric autonomous driving systems.

Hyundai will hope that Kovac can help it ramp up its robot portfolio, including the four-legged Spot, the logistics robot Stretch and the humanoid Atlas. As part of his role, he will be expected to provide strategic advice on AI and engineering initiatives focusing on the application of advanced AI and robotics in manufacturing, logistics and service operations.

At the CES event in Las Vegas earlier this month, Hyundai said that it planned to mass-produce Atlas with an annual capacity of up to 30,000 units by 2028.

Atlas, which won the Best Robot award at the show, will be deployed at Hyundai Motor Group's Metaplant America in Savannah, Georgia by 2028, initially focusing on processes with proven safety and quality benefits, such as parts sequencing.

Humanoid Market is Growing

By 2030, applications will extend to component assembly, and over time, Atlas will also take on tasks involving repetitive motions, heavy loads, and other complex operations.

Tesla expects to begin mass production of its Optimus 3 prototypes this year. Each unit is expected to cost between $20,000 and $30,000. Sales will start with businesses and then expand to consumers. They are already, apparently, working in Tesla offices and even showing guests around buildings.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Porsche Stock Records Worst Week Ever as 10% Weekly Loss Marks a New Post-IPO Low
data.description:
Porsche AG (DE:PAH3) is enduring a historic slump as its stock heads toward its most difficult week since its 2022 market debut. On Friday, shares of the iconic 911 manufacturer are on track for a 10% weekly decline, a drop that highlights the deepening crisis within the European automotive sector. This downturn is part of a multi-year slide that has seen Porsche's valuation nearly cut in half since its initial public offering.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Porsche Is in the Middle of a Major Restructuring

During a recent investment seminar in New York, Porsche's Chief Financial Officer, Jochen Breckner, reportedly offered an even "more conservative" outlook for the coming months than previously shared. Analysts from Oddo BHF noted that the company is currently in the middle of a "major restructuring" that has heavily impacted its bottom line. The consensus among financial experts is that 2026 will serve as a "transition year," though many are skeptical about how quickly profitability can return to the high levels seen pre-IPO.

Porsche Faces Headwinds from China

The most significant headwind for Porsche remains the collapse of the luxury market in China. Former CEO Oliver Blume recently admitted that the brand "got it wrong" regarding the pace of EV adoption in that region. By moving the best-selling Macan to an all-electric platform and removing combustion-engine options, Porsche inadvertently ceded market share to competitors who offer more flexible powertrain choices.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why Micron Stock (MU) Is Rallying in Pre-Market Today
data.description:
Shares of Micron Technology (MU) are up more than 6% in premarket trading today after renewed attention from Wall Street. The gains were also supported by strong earnings from semiconductor giant TSMC (TSM), which sparked a broader rally across chip stocks.

data.title: CRWV, NFLX, BA: Trump Buys Bonds Worth $51M Since November'25
data.description:
President Donald Trump has purchased additional bonds worth $51 million between November 14 and December 29, according to a White House disclosure posted Thursday. The purchases included municipal and corporate bonds, including debt from CoreWeave (CRWV), Netflix (NFLX), General Motors (GM), Boeing (BA), Occidental Petroleum (OXY), and United Rentals (URI). He also bought municipal bonds from American cities, local school districts, utilities, and hospitals.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Additionally, he acquired bonds from Block (XYZ), Sirius XM (SIRI), Whirlpool (WHR), Carnival Corp. (CCL), Victoria's Secret (VSCO), Macy's (M), and Olin Corp. (OLN).

Trump's Stakes in Policy-Tied Companies

Interestingly, most of these companies have been affected by Trump administration policies since he took office in January 2025. Trump has praised Boeing planes during trips to other countries, and highlighted their sales to airlines in Qatar, Japan, and elsewhere.

During his Ford (F) factory visit in Detroit, he praised rival General Motors's production shifts back to the U.S. as aligning with the tariff plan. Meanwhile, Netflix is battling Paramount Skydance (PSKY) for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), and regardless of who wins, it will test Trump's team on antitrust rules. Trump has stated that he will personally review the winning deal.

Why Trump Discloses His Trades

All federal elected officials and appointees are required to disclose their trades, though they do not specify exact amounts or prices. They only require broad ranges for transactions in stocks, bonds, commodity futures, and other securities. Trump reported 189 purchases and two sales (the latter totaling at least $1.3 million) between November 14 and December 29 of last year.

The disclosure, dated January 14, was approved by a White House ethics official the next day. The president also amended an earlier report, adjusting the value of four transactions.

Trump Allegedly Mixes Business with Duties

Trump's investments display how he has continued building wealth during his presidency. He has also allegedly blurred the lines between his personal business and official duties, raising questions about potential conflicts of interest. A top White House official has argued that Trump and his family do not make investment decisions personally. Independent financial managers executed the bond purchases using programs that track popular market indexes. The government ethics office also approved the reports.

Also in August 2025, Trump reported 690 transactions made since his January 2025 return to the White House, totaling at least $104 million. He disclosed further transactions in November and December totaling $106 million, including three other sales valued at $2 million.

Which Companies Are Preferred by Analysts?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to determine how the above-mentioned companies perform.

Currently, analysts have assigned a Strong Buy consensus rating on URI and BA stocks, while remaining cautiously optimistic about GM, NFLX, and CRWV shares.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Novo Nordisk Stock (NVO) Jumps as UK Clears Higher Wegovy Dose
data.description:
Novo Nordisk's (NVO) shares came alive early Friday after the British health watchdog announced it has approved a higher weekly dosage of the Danish pharmaceutical giant's Wegovy weight-loss injection.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

On Friday, the Medicines and Healthcare Products Regulatory Agency said it gave the go-ahead on January 6 for obese patients in the country to take up to 7.2 milligrams of the injection in a week, administered as three 2.4 milligram shots. However, such patients must also have a body mass index of 30kg/m² or higher and be on a reduced-calorie diet with excercise.

The approval lifts the maximum weekly approved dose from the previous 2.4 milligram and could support higher revenue per patient if uptake of the higher dose is strong. The UK permission comes several weeks after the EU medicines regulator similarly issued a positive opinion on the higher dose, paving the way for possible approval by the European Commission.

Novo Nordisk is also awaiting a similar approval from the U.S. Foods and Drug Administration following its application in late November.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: ImmunityBio Stock (IBRX) Rockets Higher after Reporting 700% Growth in Anktiva Sales
data.description:
ImmunityBio (IBRX) stock surged over 30% on Thursday after the company released preliminary 2025 results, which pointed to a 700% jump in Anktiva sales. The biotechnology company reported about $113 million in net product revenue for 2025, up from just $15 million the year before, driven by rising adoption of the therapy.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Preliminary fourth quarter 2025 revenue alone reached $38.3 million, up 20% from the previous quarter and 431% higher than the same period in 2024.

Global Approvals Add Fuel to the Rally

Beyond sales growth, ImmunityBio also announced several major regulatory wins that expand Anktiva's reach. These include:

  1. Saudi FDA approval for Anktiva in combination with checkpoint inhibitors for metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer, the first global approval for this indication. 
  2. Additional Saudi approval for Anktiva plus BCG in BCG‑unresponsive bladder cancer, adding to existing approvals in the U.S., U.K., and conditional approval in the EU.
  3. Earlier in the week, the company also released positive lung cancer data, further expanding the drug's potential market.

These milestones signal rising global confidence in the therapy and create opportunities for new revenue streams.

Here's Why Anktiva Is Driving Strong Growth

Anktiva is an IL‑15-based therapy designed to activate T cells and natural killer cells. It has delivered strong results in bladder cancer, with high response rates and long-lasting benefits. Demand has grown quickly, with unit sales up 750% in 2025 compared to 2024.

The company is also progressing ahead of schedule in its Phase 2b trial for first‑line, BCG‑naïve bladder cancer, with full enrollment expected in the first half of 2026.

Overall, the explosive sales growth, new global approvals, and strong clinical results have positioned ImmunityBio as one of the standout biotech movers of early 2026. While the IBRX stock is still volatile, as most commercial‑stage biotechs are, the momentum behind Anktiva is clearly picking up.

Is ImmunityBio a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on IBRX stock based on three Buys assigned in the past three months. Further, the average ImmunityBio price target of $7.33 per share implies 85.57% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Why Analysts Are Turning More Confident on Alibaba's AI Push
data.description:
Alibaba Group Holding (BABA) is gaining support from Wall Street as analysts grow more confident that AI could power the company's next leg of growth. Recent updates around Alibaba's Qwen app and its cloud unit show the company moving beyond simple chat tools toward AI that can handle real tasks, which analysts view as a clear advantage.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jefferies: Alibaba Is Ready for AI Agents

Jefferies analyst Thomas Chong reaffirmed a Buy rating and a $225 price target, saying Alibaba is well placed for the rise of AI agents. The 5-star analyst pointed to the latest Qwen app update, which now works closely with Alibaba's main services such as Taobao, Alipay, Fliggy, and Amap. This lets users shop, order food, plan trips, and make payments in one place.

Chong said this shows how Alibaba is turning AI into a tool that completes tasks, not just answers questions. He also highlighted strong momentum at Alibaba Cloud, noting that many business customers already use both AI models and computing services. With most companies still early in AI adoption, Jefferies sees a long runway for cloud growth ahead.

Morgan Stanley: Consumer AI Is Picking Up

Morgan Stanley analyst Gary Yu also stayed positive, keeping an Overweight rating and a $180 price target. He said consumer AI and task-based tools will likely be a major focus this year. Yu noted that the Qwen app can now handle over 400 daily tasks and has crossed 100 million monthly users in just two months.

He believes Alibaba's wide range of services gives Qwen a solid chance to become an all-in-one AI app. Morgan Stanley expects further gains in user activity and steady cloud growth to support the stock, even though higher spending to attract users could weigh on profits in the near term.

Is Alibaba Stock a Buy Now? 

Wall Street remains constructive on the stock. Alibaba stock carries a Strong Buy consensus rating, based on 13 Buy ratings and one Hold over the past three months. The average BABA price target stands at $203.66, suggesting roughly 19.15% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
